D10 Imaging L2 Understand Atom

Pinhole vs Lens

a pinhole keeps everything in focus by letting only one ray per direction through — but gathers little light; a lens floods the sensor but only one plane reads sharp

A pinhole image picks one ray per direction from the scene, so every distance is in focus and no plane is favoured. The price is brightness — almost no light gets through and what does spreads slightly by diffraction. A lens gathers a fat cone of rays from each scene point and bends them to one sensor point; near and far points form bigger cones, so only one distance is truly sharp.

Switch optics. The pinhole image is soft and dim with the near and far flowers equally in focus; the lens image is bright with one flower crisp and the other dissolved. The compromise is the whole reason lens design exists.
